test_cases.title: Successful login with valid credentials and valid TOTP code
preconditions: User account exists with a verified email and password
User has 2FA enabled and a TOTP authenticator app is configured and synchronized
steps: Send a POST request to the login endpoint with the user's valid email and password
Verify that the response indicates a TOTP prompt (e.g., HTTP 200 with a challenge token or step indicator, not a fully authenticated session)
Retrieve a current TOTP code from the configured authenticator app
Submit the TOTP code to the TOTP verification endpoint
Inspect the response for an authenticated session token or equivalent access grant
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns a successful authentication response (e.g., session token or redirect to authenticated area), confirming access is granted.

test_cases.title: TOTP prompt is displayed after valid credentials for 2FA-enabled account
preconditions: User account exists with a verified email and password
User has 2FA enabled on the account
steps: Send a POST request to the login endpoint with the user's valid email and password
Inspect the response body and status code
test_cases.expected_result: The system responds with a TOTP challenge prompt and does not grant full access or return a fully authenticated session token.

test_cases.title: Access denied when an invalid TOTP code is submitted
preconditions: User account exists with a verified email and password
User has 2FA enabled and a TOTP authenticator app is configured
User has successfully submitted valid credentials and received a TOTP prompt
steps: Submit a syntactically valid but incorrect TOTP code (e.g., '000000') to the TOTP verification endpoint
Inspect the response body and status code
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns an error response indicating the TOTP code is invalid and does not grant access or return a session token.

test_cases.title: Access denied when an expired TOTP code is submitted
preconditions: User account exists with a verified email and password
User has 2FA enabled and a TOTP authenticator app is configured
User has successfully submitted valid credentials and received a TOTP prompt
A TOTP code from a previous time window (older than the allowed TOTP validity period) is available
steps: Submit the expired TOTP code to the TOTP verification endpoint
Inspect the response body and status code
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns an error response indicating the TOTP code is expired or invalid and does not grant access or return a session token.

test_cases.title: No TOTP prompt for user without 2FA enabled
preconditions: User account exists with a verified email and password
User does NOT have 2FA enabled on the account
steps: Send a POST request to the login endpoint with the user's valid email and password
Inspect the response body and status code
test_cases.expected_result: The system grants access directly (returns a session token or equivalent) without issuing a TOTP prompt.

test_cases.title: TOTP code reuse within the same time window is rejected
preconditions: User account exists with a verified email and password
User has 2FA enabled and a TOTP authenticator app is configured
User has previously completed a successful TOTP verification with a specific code in the current time window
steps: Submit the same TOTP code that was already successfully used in the current time window to the TOTP verification endpoint
Inspect the response body and status code
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns an error response and denies access, preventing replay of an already-used TOTP code.

assumptions: The TOTP implementation follows RFC 6238 (TOTP: Time-Based One-Time Password Algorithm) with a standard 30-second time window.
After entering valid credentials, the system issues an intermediate challenge state (not a full session) before TOTP verification is complete.
The system has a defined TOTP validity window; 'expired' means outside that window.
The API exposes distinct endpoints or a multi-step flow for credential submission and TOTP submission.
TOTP codes are 6-digit numeric strings as is standard for TOTP.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the number of allowed TOTP retry attempts before lockout — rate-limiting and account lockout behavior should be clarified and tested; (2) whether the TOTP prompt has a session timeout (i.e., how long the challenge state remains valid); (3) the exact error messages to display for invalid vs. expired codes — it is unclear if these are distinct error messages or a single generic one; (4) whether TOTP code reuse (replay attack) within the same time window should be rejected — this was added as a security assumption but is not stated in the requirement; (5) recovery flows (e.g., lost authenticator) are out of scope for this requirement.
